task: Fix `.env` loading in askr init so Discord webhook URL is auto-loaded from the repo's .env file instead of requiring manual re-entry on fresh clones
discussion_summary: Friend cloned askr, ran `askr init`, entered name, then nothing happened — no Discord webhook prompt appeared. Root cause: `load_dotenv()` was searching from the working directory (where init was run) instead of from the askr repo directory itself. The fix moves the `.env` load to happen from the askr package's own directory, ensuring fresh clones automatically pick up the bundled `.env` without manual intervention.

accomplishments.what: Identified that `load_dotenv()` searches from cwd, not from askr repo directory
Modified `/Users/bippin/Desktop/askr/askr/utils/env.py` to load .env from askr repo root
Committed fix: 'fix: always load .env from askr repo'

in_progress.what: Ensure load_dotenv() resolves to askr package directory, not cwd

next_actions.action: Test fresh clone: `git clone askr`, `cd askr`, `askr init` — verify Discord webhook prompt appears and auto-loads from .env without manual entry
next_actions.why: Confirm the fix works end-to-end for the reported scenario

next_actions.action: Have friend `git pull` in their clone and re-run `askr init` to verify the prompt now appears
Verify that `env.py` correctly resolves the askr package root using `__file__` or `importlib.resources`
next_actions.why: Ensure the path resolution is robust across different installation methods (pip, git clone, editable install)

next_actions.action: Commit any remaining changes to `askr_state/implementation_state.md` and `roadmap.md`
next_actions.why: Clean up uncommitted state tracking files

decisions.decision: Move Discord webhook prompt outside the try-except block so it cannot be swallowed by exception handling
decisions.reason: Ensures the prompt always runs, even if an import or other error occurs earlier in init

decisions.decision: Load .env from askr repo directory, not from cwd
decisions.reason: Fresh clones should auto-configure without requiring users to manually copy .env or re-enter values

failed_approaches.approach: Assumed the `.env` in the repo was being loaded but the webhook URL was stale
failed_approaches.reason: User clarified that no `.env` file existed in the friend's clone directory — the real issue was `load_dotenv()` searching the wrong directory

relational_files.why: Calls `env.load()` during `askr init` — depends on correct .env resolution
Contains the Discord webhook URL that should be auto-loaded on fresh clones
